titleStmt.title: Letter from James Acheson MacCullagh, 13 January 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Acheson MacCullagh

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from James Acheson MacCullagh (1855-1918), medical inspector for the Local Government Board, referring to animal testing in the Physiological Department of University College Galway. MacCullagh reports that the animals were well cared for and comfortable.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3159/2

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
COPY
No 2337 : 1916. Sir I beg to report that under Section 10
of the Cruelty to animals Act 1876, that in
the Physiological Department, University College
Galway experiments were performed under license.
I have to report that I visited the Department
twice during the year 1915 and on the occasions
of my visits found the animals well cared for and
comfortable. They appeared to be in a healthy
condition. Your obedient Servant J. Acheson MacCullagh
Medical Inspector. Galway 13th January 1916.
